expressions.english: something you want to have; a desired item
as one wants; as one wishes
to desire eagerly; to long for; to yearn for
expressions.notes:
간절하다 = to be eager; to be desperate
간절히 is the adverb form of 간절하다.

expressions.english: What is it that you want?
Take as much as you want.
expressions.notes:
-(느)는 만큼 = as much as one + V
가져가다 = to take

expressions.english: If you don't want it, give it to me.
If you want, take this as well.
If you have something you want, hurry up and tell me.
expressions.notes:
빨르다 = to be fast
빨리 is an adverb form of 빨르다.
말하다 = to talk; to say; to speak; to tell
-어/아/여요 = imperative; Let's + V

expressions.korean: 제가 원하는 건 이런 게 아니에요.
expressions.english:
This is not the kind of thing I want.
This is not what I want.
I also don't know what I want.
expressions.notes:
뭘 = what (object)
뭘 is the contracted form of 무엇을.
모르다 = to not know

expressions.korean: 아이가 원하는 대로 하게 놔 두세요.
expressions.english: Let the kid do what he/she wants.
expressions.notes:
아이 = child; kid; baby
-게 놔 두다 = to let + person + V

expressions.english:
If you want, I can take you there by car.
If you want, I can drive you there.
expressions.notes:
차 = car
데려다 드리다 = (honorific) to take someone to + place

expressions.korean: 원하는 대로 안 된다고 화내지 마세요.
expressions.english: Don't get angry just because things don't go as you want.
expressions.notes:
화내다 = to be angry at someone; to yell at someone out of anger
-지 마세요 = (polite) Don't + V

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 항상 원하는 게 너무 많아요.
expressions.english: He always desires too much.
Everyone wants to be happy.
expressions.notes:
-들 is a suffix used to indicate plural.
누구나 = anyone, anybody
행복하다 = to be happy
-어/아/여지다 = to become + adjective

expressions.korean: 돈이 많으면 원하는 것을 다 할 수 있나요?
expressions.english: Can you do anything you want if you have a lot of money?
expressions.notes:
다 = all; every (thing)
(으)ㄹ 수 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a good chance that + S + V

expressions.english: Did you ask what kind of presents the children want?

expressions.korean: 원하는 종류의 기사만 모아서 보여주는 앱이 있대요.
expressions.english: There is a (mobile) app for gathering and displaying only the types of articles you want.
expressions.notes:
종류 = kind, sort, type (of)
기사 = (news) article
-만 = only; just
모아다 = to gather; to save up
-어/아/여서 = by + V-ing; to + V + and (then)
보여주다 = to show, display
앱 = app, application

expressions.korean: 여기는 제가 원하는 책을 싼 값에 살 수 있어서 좋아요.
expressions.english: This place is good because I can buy the books I want at a cheap price.
expressions.notes:
여기 = here; this place
싸다 = to be cheap
싼 is the adjective form of 싸다.
값 = price
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like

expressions.korean: 이 레스토랑에서는 원하는 만큼 음식을 먹을 수 있어요.
expressions.english: In this restaurant, you can eat as much food as you want.
expressions.notes:
레스토랑 = restaurant
A Korean word for a "restaurant" is "식당," but when you use "레스토랑" instead of "식당," it usually refers to a fancy or upscale restaurant that sells western-style cuisine, including Italian or French. Even if a restaurant is very fancy and expensive, if it's a Korean, Japanese, or Chinese restaurant, normally the word "레스토랑" is not used.
먹다 = to eat; to drink
